desc: HS 2521 — limestone flux. The value-chain report names Karamoja as a domestic source, consistent with this trade being almost entirely regional rather than a major standalone import.
HS 2522 — quicklime, slaked lime and hydraulic lime (distinct from limestone, HS 2521)
HS 2804 — hydrogen, argon and other industrial gases (oxygen, argon — not separately split at 4-digit level)
HS 2819 — chromium oxides and hydroxides (closest fetchable code to chromic acid/chromium salts; not an exact match — sodium dichromate is a separate HS chapter not fetched)
HS 3206 — other colouring matter and pigments, incl. titanium dioxide-based
HS 3208 — paints and varnishes based on synthetic polymers (topcoat/primer/lacquer)

HS 7202 — ferro-alloys (deoxidation/chemistry additions). Not discussed with Uganda-specific cost or supplier detail in any of the three source documents — only a generic textbook description of alloying elements.
HS 7204 — ferrous waste and scrap (EAF charge input)
HS 7207 — semi-finished products of iron/non-alloy steel (slab, billet, bloom)
HS 7208 — flat-rolled, hot-rolled, ≥600mm (the wide segment the taxation policy review confirms has no local manufacturer)
HS 7209 — flat-rolled, cold-rolled, ≥600mm
HS 7210 — flat-rolled, clad/plated/coated, ≥600mm (shared by Galvanized Sheet, Galvalume, Pre-painted Coil and Tinplate)
HS 7211 — flat-rolled, hot- or cold-rolled, <600mm (narrow strip — matches Tembo Steels' actual production width)
HS 7213 — bars and rods in coils (wire rod)
HS 7214 — other bars and rods, iron/non-alloy steel (shared by Rebar and Merchant Bar)
HS 7216 — angles, shapes and sections of iron/non-alloy steel
HS 7301 — sheet piling and welded angles/shapes/sections of iron/steel (not sheet-piling-specific)
HS 7302 — railway/tramway track construction material
HS 7304 — seamless tubes, pipes and hollow profiles of iron/steel
HS 7601 — unwrought aluminium (Al-Zn alloy bath input). Same NPA/UDC report citation as zinc above — sourced from the UK.
HS 7901 — unwrought zinc (zinc ingots for galvanizing baths). The NPA/UDC value-chain report (para 599) names zinc, aluminium and hydrochloric acid as Phase V coating-line inputs sourced from the UK.
HS 8001 — unwrought tin (tin anodes for electrolytic tinning)
HS 8545 — carbon electrodes (EAF graphite electrodes). Not mentioned in any of the three source documents despite being a known major EAF cost item elsewhere.
HS 7305+7306 — welded tubes/pipes, large- and small-diameter combined
HS 1701 — cane or beet sugar and chemically pure sucrose.
HS 1703 — molasses from extraction or refining of sugar.
HS 1704 — sugar confectionery (not cocoa-containing).
HS 2202 — waters including mineral waters, sweetened or flavoured.
HS 2207 — undenatured ethyl alcohol (≥80% vol.) and denatured alcohol.
HS 2523 — Portland cement, aluminous cement and similar hydraulic cements (incl. clinker).
HS 2701 — coal and similar solid fuels (used as kiln fuel). Uganda imports from Tanzania.
HS 2709 — crude petroleum oils. Lake Albert future feedstock.
HS 2941 — antibiotics. Key API class; QCIL produces antibiotics locally using imported APIs.
HS 3002 — human blood, vaccines, toxins (biological products). All vaccines imported.
HS 3004 — medicaments (mixed or unmixed products for therapeutic use), dosed. Uganda imports + exports both.
HS 3102 — mineral or chemical fertilizers, nitrogenous (incl. urea). All imported.
HS 3104 — mineral or chemical fertilizers; potassic (MOP). All imported.
HS 3105 — mineral or chemical fertilizers N+P+K (incl. DAP, NPK). All imported.
HS 3901 — polymers of ethylene (HDPE; LDPE; LLDPE). 100% imported.
HS 3902 — polymers of propylene (PP resin). All imported; no domestic PP resin.
HS 3917 — tubes, pipes and hoses and fittings of plastics.
HS 3920 — other plates, sheets, film of plastics (non-cellular; not reinforced).
HS 3921 — other plates, sheets, film, foil of plastics (laminates).
HS 3923 — articles for conveyance or packing of goods (bottles, bags, boxes).
HS 3924 — tableware, kitchenware, other household articles of plastics.
HS 5201 — cotton; not carded or combed. Uganda major exporter of raw lint.
HS 5205 — cotton yarn (> 85% cotton). Fine Spinners and Nytil production.
HS 6109 — T-shirts, singlets and other vests, knitted.
HS 6211 — track suits, ski suits and swimwear (incl. workwear).
HS 6305 — sacks and bags of textile materials (incl. woven PP sacks).
HS 6810 — articles of cement, concrete or artificial stone (blocks, pavers, pipes).
HS 6907 — ceramic flags and paving; hearth or wall tiles (unglazed).
HS 7005 — float glass and surface-ground / polished glass.
HS 7010 — glass vials, ampoules, bottles for pharma. All pharma glass imported.
HS 7403 — refined copper and copper alloys (unwrought), incl. cathode
HS 7407 — copper bars, rods and profiles (incl. brass rod)
HS 7408 — copper wire
HS 7604 — aluminium bars; rods and profiles
HS 7614 — stranded wire, cable, plaited bands etc. of aluminium (ACSR)
HS 8507 — electric accumulators (EV and starter batteries)
HS 8544 — insulated electric conductors and cable
HS 8702 — motor vehicles for 10 or more persons (buses and coaches)
HS 8703 — motor cars and other vehicles for persons (incl. EVs)
HS 8708 — parts and accessories of motor vehicles
HS 8711 — motorcycles
HS 8716 — trailers and semi-trailers
HS 9018 — instruments and apparatus for medical/surgical use (incl. syringes).
HS 1212 — sugarcane and other plant material; vegetable products. Fresh cane consumed entirely at Kakira / Kinyara / SCOUL mills — near-zero import trade.
HS 1515 — other fixed vegetable fats and oils, incl. castor oil (pharma excipient and industrial lubricant).
HS 1702 — other sugars (glucose syrup, fructose syrup, lactose, maltose). Glucose syrup used in confectionery; largely imported from Egypt and India.
HS 2102 — yeasts (active or inactive); prepared baking powders. Active yeast imported for ethanol fermentation at sugar mills.
HS 2503 — sulphur (all kinds except sublimed, precipitated and colloidal). Burned to generate SO2 for juice sulfitation and fertilizer processing.
HS 2510 — natural calcium phosphates; aluminium calcium phosphates (phosphate rock). Sukulu Hills deposit largely unexploited; phosphate fertilizer made from imported rock.
HS 2520 — gypsum; anhydrite; plasters of calcined gypsum. Set retarder in cement (4–6% addition); partly imported from Kenya.
HS 2601 — iron ores and concentrates. Uganda has no blast furnace — iron ore imports near-zero. Potential source: Muko/Butare deposits if a DRI plant is built.
HS 2606 — aluminium ores and concentrates (bauxite). Corrective material in cement; feedstock for aluminium smelting. Uganda has no bauxite mining or smelting.
HS 2704 — coke and semi-coke of coal; retort carbon. Metallurgical coke for blast furnace ironmaking; coke breeze for sintering.
HS 2710 — petroleum oils, refined (naphtha, diesel, fuel oil, jet fuel). Uganda's largest single import category. Kabalega refinery (Lake Albert) will displace imports.
HS 2711 — petroleum gases (LPG, propane, butane, natural gas). LPG dominates currently; Lake Albert associated gas is future industrial feedstock.
HS 2803 — carbon blacks and other forms of carbon. Used as UV stabiliser, black colourant and reinforcing agent in plastics and rubber.
HS 2806 — hydrogen chloride (HCl); chlorosulphuric acid. Pickling acid for steel descaling and copper oxide removal. No domestic HCl production.
HS 2807 — sulphuric acid; oleum. Leach medium in zinc/tin hydrometallurgy; acidulation agent for single superphosphate (SSP) fertilizer.
HS 2809 — diphosphorus pentaoxide; phosphoric acid. Juice clarification in sugar mills; feedstock for SSP phosphate fertilizer.
HS 2811 — other inorganic acids and inorganic oxygen compounds of non-metals (incl. SO2, CO2, H2S). SO2 for sugar sulfitation; CO2 for carbonation.
HS 2814 — ammonia (anhydrous or in aqueous solution). Feedstock for urea and ammonium nitrate. All imported; future domestic production from Lake Albert gas.
HS 2836 — carbonates (calcium carbonate, magnesium carbonate, sodium carbonate). CaCO3 filler in PVC pipe and rigid plastics; domestic limestone a partial substitute.
HS 2901 — acyclic hydrocarbons (ethylene, propylene, butylene, acetylene). Primary petrochemical feedstocks; Uganda imports near-zero — converted to resin overseas before export.
HS 2918 — carboxylic acids with additional oxygen function (citric acid, lactic acid, tartaric acid). Citric acid from China dominates confectionery souring.
HS 3204 — synthetic organic colouring matter; dyes (reactive, disperse, direct, vat). Entirely imported; critical bottleneck for Uganda's textile dyeing and printing sector.
HS 3207 — prepared pigments, opacifiers and colours; vitrifiable enamels and glazes; ceramic frits. Imported specialist materials for tile and ceramics production.
HS 3215 — printing ink (flexographic, gravure, offset, inkjet). Used in textile garment printing and flexible packaging. Largely imported from India and China.
HS 3302 — mixtures of odoriferous substances; flavour concentrates. Fruit-flavour concentrates for soft drinks and confectionery. All imported.
HS 3506 — prepared glues and adhesives (PU lamination adhesive, water-based adhesive). Used in flexible packaging lamination.
HS 3802 — activated carbon; activated natural mineral products. Used in sugar decolourisation, water purification and solvent recovery.
HS 3809 — finishing agents, mordants and carriers for dyeing (textile auxiliaries). Includes softeners, fixatives and optical brighteners — critical for finishing.
HS 3904 — polymers of vinyl chloride or other halogenated olefins (PVC resin). Used for cable insulation, pipe, rigid packaging. No domestic PVC resin production.
HS 3907 — polyacetals, other polyethers and polyesters in primary forms (PET bottle-grade resin; polyurethane resins; epoxy resins). All imported.
HS 3912 — cellulose and its chemical derivatives (MCC; HPMC; CMC; ethylcellulose). Pharma excipients used as fillers, binders and film-coat bases. All imported from India.
HS 3913 — natural polymers and modified natural polymers (gelatin, casein, alginic acid). Hard gelatin and HPMC capsule shells imported for pharma.
HS 3915 — waste, parings and scrap of plastics. Plastic regrind for blending with virgin resin. Uganda has a growing informal plastics recycling sector.
HS 3926 — other articles of plastics (retroreflective sheeting, tapes, safety items, buttons). Retroreflective tape for hi-vis workwear imported from China.
HS 4011 — new pneumatic tyres of rubber. Uganda has no domestic tyre manufacturing; all tyres imported primarily from China, India and South Africa.
HS 4015 — articles of vulcanised rubber (gloves, stoppers, sheeting). Rubber stoppers and closures for pharma vials; all imported.
HS 4016 — other articles of vulcanised rubber (gaskets, seals, anti-vibration mounts). Automotive seals and gaskets; all imported.
HS 5402 — synthetic filament yarn (polyester sewing thread; nylon yarn; textured polyester). Sewing thread is a critical garment input; all imported from China and India.
HS 5503 — synthetic staple fibres (polyester staple fibre; acrylic staple). PSF imported for blended yarn production at Fine Spinners and Nytil.
HS 5504 — artificial staple fibres (viscose / rayon staple; modal; lyocell). Viscose fibre imported for blended yarn; no domestic viscose production.
HS 5513 — woven fabrics of synthetic staple fibres (< 85% by weight; mixed with cotton). Poly-cotton workwear fabric largely imported from China and Pakistan.
HS 5807 — labels and badges of textile materials (woven brand labels; care labels). Woven and printed labels imported for garment production.
HS 6006 — other knitted or crocheted fabrics (finished knitted fabric; dyed or printed). Imported for cut-and-sew garments; some produced by Nytil.
HS 7007 — safety glass (toughened tempered or laminated). Windscreens and side glass for vehicle assembly; all imported.
HS 7019 — glass fibres and articles thereof (fibreglass / GRP panels; chopped strand mat). Used in bodywork of buses and special-purpose vehicles.
HS 7217 — wire of iron or non-alloy steel (galvanised steel wire; armour wire for cables). SWA cable armouring; no domestic fine-wire drawing capacity.
HS 7404 — copper waste and scrap. Copper scrap for remelting in brass and wire rod production.
HS 7607 — aluminium foil (≤ 0.2 mm thick). Barrier layer in flexible packaging and moisture-barrier tape in cables. No domestic foil production in Uganda.
HS 7801 — unwrought lead (lead ingots). Used in brass production (1–3% Pb machinability additive) and battery plates.
HS 8311 — wire, rods and electrodes for welding or brazing. MIG wire and covered electrodes for steel fabrication and vehicle bodywork. Partly sourced domestically.
HS 8407 — spark-ignition reciprocating or rotary internal combustion piston engines. CKD motorcycle and small vehicle engines imported from China and India.
HS 8501 — electric motors and generators (incl. traction motors for EVs and general industrial drives).
HS 8706 — chassis fitted with engines for motor vehicles. Semi-knocked-down bus and truck chassis imported for local bodywork.
HS 8714 — parts and accessories for motorcycles and bicycles (frames, forks, handlebars, suspension components). CKD motorcycle parts from China and India.
HS 2621 — slag and ash; incl. seaweed ash and natural pozzolana (volcanic ash used as supplementary cementitious material / cement extender).
HS 2529 — feldspar; leucite; nepheline; fluorspar (ceramic body fluxing agent that reduces sintering temperature).
HS 3821 — prepared culture media for development of micro-organisms and plant, human or animal cells. Used in biopharmaceutical production.
HS 3824 — prepared binders for foundry moulds; chemical products and preparations NEC (incl. concrete superplasticisers, admixtures, surface treatment agents).
HS 9607 — slide fasteners (zip fasteners) and parts thereof. Zips for workwear and garments; almost entirely imported from China.
HS 2608 — zinc ores and concentrates (including roasted zinc calcine). Feedstock for zinc smelting; Uganda imports small volumes, primarily through the EAC corridor from DRC and Zambia producers.
HS 2609 — tin ores and concentrates (cassiterite — SnO2). Rwanda is the dominant EAC exporter; Uganda imports concentrate for downstream processing.
HS 2713 — petroleum coke, petroleum bitumen and other residues of petroleum oils. Petroleum coke used as reductant in smelting and as fuel for high-temperature furnaces.
HS 2815 — sodium hydroxide (caustic soda); potassium hydroxide; peroxides of sodium/potassium. Caustic soda is the digestion medium in the Bayer alumina process and a general industrial alkali.
HS 2818 — artificial corundum; aluminium oxide (alumina); aluminium hydroxide. Alumina (Al2O3) is the electrolytic feedstock for primary aluminium smelting via the Hall-Héroult process.
HS 2827 — chlorides, chloride oxides and chloride hydroxides (including ammonium chloride). Ammonium chloride is the standard pre-flux agent in hot-dip galvanizing lines.

HS 3403 — lubricating preparations (including cutting-oil preparations, anti-rust/corrosion preparations, mould-release preparations). Rolling lubricants and mill emulsions are entirely imported; no domestic production identified.
